behaviors.description: Scoring model assigns the correct risk score when a transaction exceeds the configured amount threshold for its merchant category.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic rule evaluation (amount vs category threshold plus score calculation) inside the scoring component. The failure is in local business logic, not in cross-module wiring, real infra, or model non-determinism.
behaviors.test_description: Call the scoring function with an in-memory config map (merchant-category thresholds) and a transaction just above threshold; assert the exact numeric score contribution and final score.

behaviors.description: Risk model identifies a fraudulent transaction pattern (rapid small purchases from different geographies) even when no single rule threshold is exceeded.
behaviors.justification: The behavior asserts model prediction quality/pattern recognition, which depends on real ML model behavior rather than deterministic local logic. Mocking the model would hide the true failure mode.
behaviors.test_description: Send representative transaction-context sequences to the real risk model endpoint (or real served model) and assert fraud probability/classification crosses the fraud threshold for the pattern, across repeated runs with acceptance bands.

behaviors.description: Velocity check in scoring model correctly counts number of transactions within configured time window per user.
behaviors.justification: Real failures commonly occur at the boundary between velocity logic and transaction-history retrieval/filtering (time window, ordering, per-user partition). A pure unit test of counting logic can miss wiring/query bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Using local persistence (test DB or repository implementation), seed timestamped transactions for multiple users around the window boundary, run scoring service end-to-end for one user, and assert the computed velocity count used in scoring.

behaviors.description: Transaction flagged by risk model but not scoring model is still held for review (OR gate, not AND).
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is model-output handoff into decision orchestration. You need both module outputs combined in the real decision path to catch OR/AND wiring mistakes.
behaviors.test_description: Wire scoring and risk outputs through the review-decision service with deterministic stubs: scoring returns not-flagged, risk returns flagged; assert final disposition is 'hold for review'.

behaviors.description: Combined output includes both numeric risk score and risk-model fraud probability so reviewer can see which model flagged and why.
behaviors.justification: This checks contract assembly across components (scoring output + risk output + decision metadata). A single-function unit test can miss dropped/mis-mapped fields during module composition.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the service response-building path with both model outputs present and verify the returned payload/schema includes `risk_score`, `fraud_probability`, and per-model flag/reason fields with correct values.
